subject: docs: Phase 6 — vanilla-DaisyUI sweep documentation
body:
Updates CLAUDE.md + DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md + SESSION_NOTES.md +
HISTORY.md to reflect the completed 8-phase vanilla-DaisyUI sweep.

CLAUDE.md "Frontend: Styles and Scripts":
  - Replaced the 6-tier precedence rule with a vanilla-only policy:
    only DaisyUI component classes + DaisyUI semantic tokens + stock
    Tailwind v4 utilities. Zero custom CSS, zero @theme extensions,
    zero @utility directives, zero arbitrary bracket values, zero
    brand hex literals.
  - Listed the two remaining exceptions: DebugPill.jsx inline hex
    (isolated root that survives CSS failure) and themes.js:137
    fallback `#808080` (single safety-net).

DAISYUI_V5_NOTES.md:
  - "Project conventions" rewritten as vanilla-only policy. Migration
    path for new patterns: stock utility → DaisyUI token → component
    class → runtime CSS var resolution → out of scope.
  - "Tag chips" section rewritten to document the 7-variant DaisyUI
    badge system (success/error/warning/secondary/info/accent/neutral)
    replacing the deleted 34-hex palette.
  - "Custom CSS policy: zero" section enumerates every deleted class
    and its migration target.
  - "Previously deliberately NOT used — now used" section flips
    `drawer` and `collapse` from the rejected list to adopted,
    with rationale for why the previous objections were overblown.
  - `dropdown` + `menu` stay in "deliberately NOT used" — HamburgerMenu
    still uses React-state disclosure for the same reasons.
  - Chart.js dataset colours section rewritten for runtime
    getComputedStyle resolution of 8 semantic vars.
  - Toast convention updated to note `z-[var(--z-toast)]` is the only
    permitted arbitrary bracket (design-token CSS var reference).
  - Drawer + Collapse conventions added with full JSX examples.

HISTORY.md:
  - Added top-level "Vanilla-DaisyUI sweep — 8 phases, zero custom CSS"
    entry with per-phase commit hashes, visual regressions, and URL
    params removed.

SESSION_NOTES.md:
  - Added current-state block at the top summarising the 8-phase sweep
    and final state (zero custom classes, full DaisyUI adoption).

Verified: vite build clean (2.66s), 60/60 tests pass, final audit
confirms:
  - 0 custom class primary rules in dashboard/styles.css
  - 0 arbitrary `text-[Npx]` usages in dashboard/js
  - 0 hex colour literals in chartColors.js or utils.js (tag palette)

Branch state after this commit: the full vanilla-DaisyUI sweep is
complete. The dashboard uses exclusively DaisyUI components, DaisyUI
semantic tokens, and stock Tailwind utilities.

Tags: docs, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
title: Phase 6 — vanilla-DaisyUI sweep documentation
